2604.06770.title: FlowExtract: Procedural Knowledge Extraction from Maintenance Flowcharts
2604.06770.paper_summary: Maintenance procedures in manufacturing facilities are often documented as flowcharts in static PDFs or scanned images. They encode procedural knowledge essential for asset lifecycle management, yet inaccessible to modern operator support systems. Vision-language models, the dominant paradigm for image understanding, struggle to reconstruct connection topology from such diagrams. We present FlowExtract, a pipeline for extracting directed graphs from ISO 5807-standardized flowcharts. The system separates element detection from connectivity reconstruction, using YOLOv8 and EasyOCR for standard domain-aligned node detection and text extraction, combined with a novel edge detection method that analyzes arrowhead orientations and traces connecting lines backward to source nodes. Evaluated on industrial troubleshooting guides, FlowExtract achieves very high node detection and substantially outperforms vision-language model baselines on edge extraction, offering organizations a practical path toward queryable procedural knowledge representations. The implementation is available athttps://github.com/guille-gil/FlowExtract.
2604.06770.paper_summary_zh: 製造設施中的維護程序通常以靜態 PDF 或掃描圖像的流程圖形式記錄。這些流程圖編碼了資產生命周期管理所需的程序知識，但對現代操作員支持系統來說卻無法訪問。視覺-語言模型，作為圖像理解的主導範式，難以從這些圖表中重建連接拓撲。我們提出了 FlowExtract，一個從 ISO 5807 標準化流程圖中提取有向圖的管道。該系統將元素檢測與連接重建分開，使用 YOLOv8 和 EasyOCR 進行標準領域對齊的節點檢測和文本提取，並結合一種新穎的邊緣檢測方法，分析箭頭方向並向後追蹤連接線到源節點。在工業故障排除指南上進行評估時，FlowExtract 實現了非常高的節點檢測率，並在邊緣提取上大幅超越視覺-語言模型基準，為組織提供了一條可查詢的程序知識表示的實用途徑。實現可在 https://github.com/guille-gil/FlowExtract 獲得。
2604.06770.author: Guillermo Gil de Avalle et.al.
2604.06770.authors: Guillermo Gil de Avalle, Laura Maruster, Eric Sloot, Christos Emmanouilidis
